16: // sessionConnectionCleanupJob defines a periodic job that monitors workers for
17: // loss of connection and terminates connections on workers that have
18: // not sent a heartbeat in a significant period of time.
19: //
20: // Relevant connections are simply marked as disconnected in the
21: // database. Connections will be independently terminated by the
22: // worker, or the event of a synchronization issue between the two,
23: // the controller will win out and order that the connections be
24: // closed on the worker.
25: type sessionConnectionCleanupJob struct {
26: 	connectionRepoFn common.ConnectionRepoFactory
27: 
28: 	// The amount of time to give disconnected workers before marking
29: 	// their connections as closed.
30: 	gracePeriod time.Duration
31: 
32: 	// The total number of connections closed in the last run.
33: 	totalClosed int
34: }
35: 
36: // newSessionConnectionCleanupJob instantiates the session cleanup job.
37: func newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(
38: 	connectionRepoFn common.ConnectionRepoFactory,
39: 	gracePeriod time.Duration,
40: ) (*sessionConnectionCleanupJob, error) {
41: 	const op = "controller.newNewSessionConnectionCleanupJob"
42: 	switch {
43: 	case connectionRepoFn == nil:
44: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing connectionRepoFn")
45: 	case gracePeriod < session.DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace:
46: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(
47: 			errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprintf("invalid gracePeriod, must be greater than %s", session.DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace))
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	return &sessionConnectionCleanupJob{
51: 		connectionRepoFn: connectionRepoFn,
52: 		gracePeriod:      gracePeriod,
53: 	}, nil
54: }
55: 
56: // Name returns a short, unique name for the job.
57: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Name() string { return "session_cleanup" }
58: 
59: // Description returns the description for the job.
60: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Description() string {
61: 	return "Clean up session connections from disconnected workers"
62: }
63: 
64: // NextRunIn returns the next run time after a job is completed.
65: //
66: // The next run time is defined for sessionConnectionCleanupJob as one second.
67: // This is because the job should run continuously to terminate
68: // connections as soon as a worker has not reported in for a long
69: // enough time. Only one job will ever run at once, so there is no
70: // reason why it cannot run again immediately.
71: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) NextRunIn(_ context.Context) (time.Duration, error) {
72: 	return time.Second, nil
73: }
74: 
75: // Status returns the status of the running job.
76: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Status() scheduler.JobStatus {
77: 	return scheduler.JobStatus{
78: 		Completed: j.totalClosed,
79: 		Total:     j.totalClosed,
80: 	}
81: }
82: 
83: // Run executes the job.
84: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
85: 	const op = "controller.(sessionConnectionCleanupJob).Run"
86: 	j.totalClosed = 0
87: 
88: 	// Load repos.
89: 	connectionRepo, err := j.connectionRepoFn()
90: 	if err != nil {
91: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error getting session repo"))
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	// Run the atomic dead worker cleanup job.
95: 	results, err := connectionRepo.CloseConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, j.gracePeriod)
96: 	if err != nil {
97: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	for _, result := range results {
101: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("worker has not reported status within acceptable grace period, all connections closed"),
102: 			event.WithInfo(
103: 				"private_id", result.ServerId,
104: 				"update_time", result.LastUpdateTime,
105: 				"grace_period_seconds", j.gracePeriod.Seconds(),
106: 				"number_connections_closed", result.NumberConnectionsClosed,
107: 			))
108: 
109: 		j.totalClosed += result.NumberConnectionsClosed
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	return nil
113: }

69: 		} else {

71: 		}
72: 	}

382: func (c *Controller) Shutdown() error {
383: 	const op = "controller.(Controller).Shutdown"
384: 	if !c.started.Load() {
385: 		event.WriteSysEvent(context.TODO(), op, "already shut down, skipping")
386: 	}
387: 	defer c.started.Store(false)
388: 	c.baseCancel()
389: 	if err := c.stopServersAndListeners(); err != nil {
390: 		return fmt.Errorf("error stopping controller servers and listeners: %w", err)
391: 	}

393: 	c.tickerWg.Wait()

15: // SessionCleanupJob defines a periodic job that monitors workers for
16: // loss of connection and terminates connections on workers that have
17: // not sent a heartbeat in a significant period of time.
18: //
19: // Relevant connections are simply marked as disconnected in the
20: // database. Connections will be independently terminated by the
21: // worker, or the event of a synchronization issue between the two,
22: // the controller will win out and order that the connections be
23: // closed on the worker.
24: type SessionCleanupJob struct {
25: 	connectionRepoFn common.ConnectionRepoFactory
26: 
27: 	// The amount of time to give disconnected workers before marking
28: 	// their connections as closed.
29: 	gracePeriod time.Duration
30: 
31: 	// The total number of connections closed in the last run.
32: 	totalClosed int
33: }
34: 
35: // NewSessionConnectionCleanupJob instantiates the session cleanup job.
36: func NewSessionConnectionCleanupJob(
37: 	connectionRepoFn common.ConnectionRepoFactory,
38: 	gracePeriod time.Duration,
39: ) (*SessionCleanupJob, error) {
40: 	const op = "session.NewSessionConnectionCleanupJob"
41: 	switch {
42: 	case connectionRepoFn == nil:
43: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing connectionRepoFn")
44: 	case gracePeriod < DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace:
45: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(
46: 			errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprintf("invalid gracePeriod, must be greater than %s", DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace))
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	return &SessionCleanupJob{
50: 		connectionRepoFn: connectionRepoFn,
51: 		gracePeriod:      gracePeriod,
52: 	}, nil
53: }
54: 
55: // Name returns a short, unique name for the job.
56: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) Name() string { return "session_cleanup" }
57: 
58: // Description returns the description for the job.
59: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) Description() string {
60: 	return "Clean up session connections from disconnected workers"
61: }
62: 
63: // NextRunIn returns the next run time after a job is completed.
64: //
65: // The next run time is defined for SessionCleanupJob as one second.
66: // This is because the job should run continuously to terminate
67: // connections as soon as a worker has not reported in for a long
68: // enough time. Only one job will ever run at once, so there is no
69: // reason why it cannot run again immediately.
70: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) NextRunIn(_ context.Context) (time.Duration, error) {
71: 	return time.Second, nil
72: }
73: 
74: // Status returns the status of the running job.
75: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) Status() scheduler.JobStatus {
76: 	return scheduler.JobStatus{
77: 		Completed: j.totalClosed,
78: 		Total:     j.totalClosed,
79: 	}
80: }
81: 
82: // Run executes the job.
83: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
84: 	const op = "session.(SessionCleanupJob).Run"
85: 	j.totalClosed = 0
86: 
87: 	// Load repos.
88: 	connectionRepo, err := j.connectionRepoFn()
89: 	if err != nil {
90: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error getting session repo"))
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	// Run the atomic dead worker cleanup job.
94: 	results, err := connectionRepo.CloseConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, j.gracePeriod)
95: 	if err != nil {
96: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	for _, result := range results {
100: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("worker has not reported status within acceptable grace period, all connections closed"),
101: 			event.WithInfo(
102: 				"private_id", result.ServerId,
103: 				"update_time", result.LastUpdateTime,
104: 				"grace_period_seconds", j.gracePeriod.Seconds(),
105: 				"number_connections_closed", result.NumberConnectionsClosed,
106: 			))
107: 
108: 		j.totalClosed += result.NumberConnectionsClosed
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	return nil
112: }

1: package session
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	stderrors "errors"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"time"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/observability/event"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/scheduler"
12: )
13: 
14: // SessionCleanupJob defines a periodic job that monitors workers for
15: // loss of connection and terminates connections on workers that have
16: // not sent a heartbeat in a significant period of time.
17: //
18: // Relevant connections are simply marked as disconnected in the
19: // database. Connections will be independently terminated by the
20: // worker, or the event of a synchronization issue between the two,
21: // the controller will win out and order that the connections be
22: // closed on the worker.
23: type SessionCleanupJob struct {
24: 	connectionRepoFn ConnectionRepoFactory
25: 
26: 	// The amount of time to give disconnected workers before marking
27: 	// their connections as closed.
28: 	gracePeriod time.Duration
29: 
30: 	// The total number of connections closed in the last run.
31: 	totalClosed int
32: }
33: 
34: // NewSessionConnectionCleanupJob instantiates the session cleanup job.
35: func NewSessionConnectionCleanupJob(
36: 	connectionRepoFn ConnectionRepoFactory,
37: 	gracePeriod time.Duration,
38: ) (*SessionCleanupJob, error) {
39: 	const op = "session.NewSessionConnectionCleanupJob"
40: 	switch {
41: 	case connectionRepoFn == nil:
42: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing connectionRepoFn")
43: 	case gracePeriod < DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace:
44: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(
45: 			errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprintf("invalid gracePeriod, must be greater than %s", DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace))
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	return &SessionCleanupJob{
49: 		connectionRepoFn: connectionRepoFn,
50: 		gracePeriod:      gracePeriod,
51: 	}, nil
52: }
53: 
54: // Name returns a short, unique name for the job.
55: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) Name() string { return "session_cleanup" }
56: 
57: // Description returns the description for the job.
58: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) Description() string {
59: 	return "Clean up session connections from disconnected workers"
60: }
61: 
62: // NextRunIn returns the next run time after a job is completed.
63: //
64: // The next run time is defined for SessionCleanupJob as one second.
65: // This is because the job should run continuously to terminate
66: // connections as soon as a worker has not reported in for a long
67: // enough time. Only one job will ever run at once, so there is no
68: // reason why it cannot run again immediately.
69: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) NextRunIn(_ context.Context) (time.Duration, error) {
70: 	return time.Second, nil
71: }
72: 
73: // Status returns the status of the running job.
74: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) Status() scheduler.JobStatus {
75: 	return scheduler.JobStatus{
76: 		Completed: j.totalClosed,
77: 		Total:     j.totalClosed,
78: 	}
79: }
80: 
81: // Run executes the job.
82: func (j *SessionCleanupJob) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
83: 	const op = "session.(SessionCleanupJob).Run"
84: 	j.totalClosed = 0
85: 
86: 	// Load repos.
87: 	connectionRepo, err := j.connectionRepoFn()
88: 	if err != nil {
89: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error getting session repo"))
90: 	}
91: 
92: 	// Run the atomic dead worker cleanup job.
93: 	results, err := connectionRepo.CloseConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, j.gracePeriod)
94: 	if err != nil {
95: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	for _, result := range results {
99: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("worker has not reported status within acceptable grace period, all connections closed"),
100: 			event.WithInfo(
101: 				"private_id", result.ServerId,
102: 				"update_time", result.LastUpdateTime,
103: 				"grace_period_seconds", j.gracePeriod.Seconds(),
104: 				"number_connections_closed", result.NumberConnectionsClosed,
105: 			))
106: 
107: 		j.totalClosed += result.NumberConnectionsClosed
108: 	}
109: 
110: 	return nil
111: }
